body: ## Purpose Use the recently-added ClimaLand convenience constructors to simplify setting up the integrated land model Most parameters remain unchanged now that we use the defaults, with the exception of: - `ac_canopy`: previously 2.5e3 --> now 2e3 - `K_sat` (canopy): previously 5e-9 --> now 7e-8 The debug plots have changed slightly in this PR, which I expect is due to these parameter changes (see examples below). Specifically these variables have changed: canopy temperature, snow water equivalent, momentum fluxes, moisture flux, LHF. Note: We do _not_ update to use `LandSimulation` here - see #1479 ## Content - [x] use default `LandModel` constructor - [x] use `LandSimulation` object, and the setup contained in that constructor - [x] remove `stepper` argument to ClimaCoupler's `ClimaLandSimulation` since it was never overwritten - [x] update `step!` function to be compatible with ITime as well as float time Closes #1473 ## Debug plot changes in AMIP + ED only + integrated land I don't know why the plot titles are messed up :') Note the changes in canopy temperature (bottom left panel) and SWE (bottom right panel). The colorbars have also changed so direct comparisons are difficult. #### Before this PR ([build](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/6908#01991700-770f-4286-a92c-40218a2fa56b)) Land fields Coupler fluxes 72d-4826-99f4-f5d9239e159c" /> #### After this PR ([build](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/6919#019935b5-3729-48eb-8c42-52b16b43f40f)) Land fields Coupler fluxes ---- - [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: use default land constructors
